WAIT FOR D. G. CORBIN

pages.raw_text:
Prominent officials have been In
formed during the past few days
that deal has been completed be-
tween the Canadian Pacific railway
and the o. r. & N. officials whereby
the former road Is to build a branch
line into Spokane and is to have an
outlet to Portland and the southeast
over the o. r. G. N. and Union Pa
cific tracks.


A person identified with the Harri-
man interests has given out the news
in Spokane.


By the arrangements the two roads
are to have union depot in Spokane.
The site desired for the new depot is
on the north bank of the Spokane
river near Howard street.


Negotiations are now under way
for securing the right of way to the
depot site. The o. R. & N. yards
will be used for freightage purposes.


1.-THE men interested in this plan
are waiting the return Of d. c. Cor
bin from Olympia" said a city Of
fetal. "As soon as Mr.. Corbin returns
active steps are to be taken in COM
plotting the arrangement. Mr.. Corbin
will be in town only a few days, and
will then go to New York and Wash
ington in the interest of the pro-
ject, he being the prime mover in the
arrangements that are being made"
